name: Hook
description:
Library component. Models a Claude Code session lifecycle hook -- one of the events that fires at an agent's session at well-defined moments (PreCompact, UserPromptSubmit, ToolUse, SessionStart). This first cut models PreCompact specifically, since that is the hook PR 745 (gastownhall/gascity#744) found a bug in. The composing application binds the AGENT port to whichever agent receives the event (Polecat, Mayor, Witness, etc.). gastown-guide does not model hooks at all -- they are substrate-layer events that no spec there describes.

Single-fire variant. For multi-cycle substrate models, use `HookCycler` (lib/hookcycler.kinner.json) instead.

AGENT.description: fires the hook event at the bound agent (the Claude session targeted by this hook). Tagged with 'PreCompact' so the bound receiver can pattern-match on this specific lifecycle event.

actors.name: Hook
actors.owner: Lifecycle event firer. Real implementation: internal/hooks/config.go (type Hook). Each Hook instance fires once when its lifecycle moment is reached; the bound agent receives the event via the AGENT port and is responsible for handling. Generalization to multiple hook types and multi-fire (a single hook firing many times across an agent's lifetime) is in HookCycler.

triples.event: terminal: hook fires exactly once per lifecycle moment
